name: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
summary: India's first comprehensive digital personal data protection law. It regulates the processing of digital personal data within India and cross-border processing of Indian citizens' data. Enforcement is phased, with full consent and processing obligations taking effect by May 2027.
keyRequirements: Obtain free, specific, informed, and unambiguous consent with clear privacy notices
Mandatory data breach notification to the Data Protection Board and affected individuals
Data principals have rights to access, correction, erasure, and grievance redressal
Heightened protections for children's data including verifiable parental consent
Data fiduciaries must implement reasonable security safeguards
Restrictions on cross-border data transfers to notified countries
penalties: Maximum penalty of INR 250 crore (~USD 30 million) per contravention. Specific penalties: up to INR 200 crore for failure to notify breaches, up to INR 200 crore for children's data violations. Enforced by the Data Protection Board of India (DPBI).
